design_philosophy.core_loop: Select a sector from six available stages, navigate platforming challenges, defeat sector boss, retrieve data tape, repeat until all six tapes collected, then face final boss in control center
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Navigating hostile alien-infested sectors with limited health while managing boomerang upgrades and finding agent contacts to access secure areas
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Elite security officer NOVA (resembling an action hero) using a mystical power boomerang to systematically dismantle an alien occupation of a space colony
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Aggressive localization philosophy: completely restructured level design, redesigned protagonist from cartoony robot to muscular action hero, added narrative depth through in-game cutscenes and agent NPCs, rebalanced gameplay mechanics, and enhanced visual presentation compared to Power Blazer. Game explicitly positions itself as 'Mega Man-style' with differentiation through 8-directional boomerang attacks and enforced death-on-screen-fall mechanic (unique to Power Blade; sequels allowed fallback)

difficulty.lives_system: Standard life meter that decreases on enemy contact or pit falls; multiple lives with game over on depletion
difficulty.continues_system: Continue feature allows restarting at the beginning of the level where the player lost all lives
difficulty.checkpointing: Password system allows restarting at specific checkpoints at any time
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through stage select (non-linear progression of six sectors in any order) and password-based checkpointing system; player can choose difficulty ordering and retry individual sectors

progression.upgrade_system: Power-up collection system: upgradeable boomerang power, boomerang range, number of simultaneous boomerangs; Power Suit item equips automatically to enable 8-directional energy blast attacks through most surfaces
progression.unlock_mechanism: Agent contact required in each sector to obtain ID card for database security room access; all six sector tapes required to unlock Master Computer control center finale
replayability_hooks: Stage select allows any order completion strategy
Password system enables save-scumming and checkpoint experimentation
Different upgrade paths based on power-up collection order
Hidden sound test (hold Left+A+B on Controller 2, press Start on Controller 1)

world_building_techniques: Pre-game title screen intro cutscene (added in Power Blade localization) establishing alien invasion of New Earth Master Computer
In-game dialogue with agent NPCs providing story context and access mechanics
Stage intro screens with narrative framing
Epilogue cutscene after final boss defeat

Environmental context: six named sectors (Life Research, Energy Development, Military Base, Weather Control Research, Residential Area, Central Control Center) suggesting colony infrastructure narrative

boss_design.philosophy: Each of six sectors contains a 'security guard' boss representing alien occupation forces; final boss is the alien overlord (identified as David Uncle with transformation sequence)
notable_bosses: Sector-specific security guard bosses (6 total, names not documented in sources)
Final Boss: Alien Overlord (David Uncle) - features transformation phase with unique music track (BGM0C)

faction_indicators: NOVA as authorized security officer aligned with Master Computer/New Earth colony
Alien invaders as opposing faction occupying six sectors
Agent NPCs as colony resistance/collaborators providing tactical support
danger_signals: Aliens occupying each sector indicating active invasion state
Master Computer offline/compromised indicating infrastructure collapse risk
Time limit mechanic (life meter decreases on timeout) signaling urgency
environmental_storytelling: Six named sectors reflect functional colony structure: Life Research, Energy Development, Military Base, Weather Control Research, Residential Area, Central Control Center
Heavily guarded access points (agent contact requirement) indicating occupying force control
Database security rooms as defended infrastructure nodes
Control center as command nexus requiring all six tape units for access

sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor (gameplay mechanics, localization philosophy, character design intent, boss naming, narrative delivery details)
wikipedia - Power Blade (core gameplay loop, progression structure, upgrade system, difficulty mechanics, plot summary, localization extent)
